claim: For all odd integers n, n^4 - 1 is divisible by 16.
For all odd integers n, n^2 - 1 is divisible by 8.
For all odd integers n, n^4 is congruent to 1 modulo 16.
For all odd integers n, n^2 + 1 is congruent to 2 modulo 8.
For all odd integers n, n^2 - 1 is divisible by 24 if and only if n is not divisible by 3.
For all odd integers n, n^4 - 1 is divisible by 80.
For all odd integers n, n^4 is congruent to 1 modulo 5.
For all odd integers n not divisible by 5, n^4 - 1 is divisible by 80.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^4 - 1,2) is at least 4.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^4 - 1,2) is at least 5 whenever n is congruent to 1 modulo 4.
For all odd integers n, gcd(n-1,n+1)=2.
For all integers n, gcd(n-1,n+1) divides 2.
For all odd integers n, (n^4 - 1)/16 is an integer.
For all odd integers n, (n^4 - 1)/16 is even whenever n is congruent to 1 modulo 8.
For all odd integers n, sigma(abs(n^2-1)) is even.
For all odd integers n, tau(abs(n^2-1)) is even.
For all odd integers n, n^8 - 1 is divisible by 32.
For all odd integers n, n^8 is congruent to 1 modulo 32.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^8-1,2) is at least 5.
For all odd integers n, n^6 - 1 is divisible by 8.
For all odd integers n not divisible by 3, n^6 is congruent to 1 modulo 9.
For all odd integers n, n^6 - 1 is divisible by 63 whenever n is not divisible by 3.
For all odd integers n, phi(abs(n^4-1)) is divisible by 8.
For all odd integers n, phi(abs(n^4-1)) is divisible by 16.
For all odd integers n, (n-1)(n+1) is divisible by 8.
For all odd integers n, (n-1)(n+1) is divisible by 3 whenever n is not divisible by 3.
For all odd integers n not divisible by 3, n^2 - 1 is divisible by 24.
For all odd integers n, n^4+14n^2+1 is divisible by 16.
For all odd integers n, n^4+1 is congruent to 2 modulo 16.
For all odd integers n, gcd(n^2-1,n^4-1)=abs(n^2-1).
For all odd integers n, n^2 - 1 divides n^4 - 1.
For all odd integers n, n^4-1 factors as four consecutive parity-alternating linear factors over integers.
For all odd integers n, n^2+1 is even.
For all odd integers n, gcd(n^2+1,n)=1.
For all odd integers n, gcd(n^2+1,n-1) divides 2.
For all odd integers n, gcd(n^2+1,n+1) divides 2.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^4-1,5) is at least 1 whenever n is not divisible by 5.
For all odd integers n not divisible by 5, n^20 is congruent to 1 modulo 25.
For all odd integers n, abs(n^4-1) is composite whenever abs(n)>1.
For all odd prime integers n, n^4 - 1 is composite.
For all odd integers n, sigma(abs(n^4-1)) exceeds abs(n^4-1).
For all odd integers n with abs(n)>1, tau(abs(n^4-1)) is at least 6.
For all odd integers n, phi(abs(n^2-1)) is divisible by 4.
For all odd integers n with abs(n)>1, sigma(abs(n^2-1)) is even.
For all odd integers n, gcd(n-1,n^2+1)=2.
For all odd integers n, gcd(n+1,n^2+1)=2.
For all odd integers n, n^4-1 is divisible by 3 whenever n is not divisible by 3.
For all odd integers n not divisible by 15, n^4-1 is divisible by 240.
For all odd integers n, n^8-1 is divisible by 255 whenever n is coprime to 15.
For all odd integers n, n^4+4 is congruent to 5 modulo 16.
For all odd integers n, n^12 is congruent to 1 modulo 16.
For all odd integers n, (n^2-1)/8 is an integer.
For all odd integers n, (n^2-1)/8 is odd whenever n is congruent to 3 modulo 8.
For all odd integers n, (n^2-1)/8 is odd whenever n is congruent to 5 modulo 8.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^4-1,2) equals vp(n-1,2)+vp(n+1,2)+1.
